origin.note: Lerner noticed 'renewing the people' as an interesting translation choice during guided reading of da-xue-zhangju-001. The core confusion: 亲 (near/love) vs 新 (renew) — Zhu Xi reads 亲 as 新, Wang Yangming reads 亲 literally. This is one of the most contested single-character readings in the Confucian canon.

layers.gloss_en: renew the people (Zhu Xi: 亲→新) / be near the people (Wang Yangming: 亲 literal)
layers.translation_en: Zhu Xi reads this as 'renew the people,' taking 亲 as 新. Wang Yangming reads 亲 literally as 'love/be near the people.' The debate turns on whether the program of great learning is about transforming others or extending virtue toward them.

notes: Phrase-level entry: the confusion is about the reading of 亲 in context, not about the character alone.
Zhu Xi's justification: parallel passage in the Da Xue commentary quotes the Odes and Shangshu using 新.
Wang Yangming's counter: 亲 should be read as written — extending bright virtue toward people, not refashioning them.
The graph: 亲 is a hand beside a tree — intimate, near.

Learner insight (guided reading session): 亲 also means 'parent / family' (as in 父母亲, 双亲). The semantic arc runs near → intimate → family → parent. This supports reading 亲民 as 'be as family with the populace' — extending familial nearness outward to all people. Aligns with Wang Yangming's reading and reads the character as it sits, without requiring Zhu Xi's external commentary justification.
